headline: FREEPORT GRASBERG BLOWS UP: Indonesia Copper Output Collapses 72% YoY Despite Q1 Earnings Beat
snippet: Freeport-McMoRan beat Q1 2026 EPS estimates ($0.57 vs $0.46 expected) but shares dropped sharply after Indonesia's Grasberg — one of the world's largest copper deposits — saw output plunge from 290 million to 82 million pounds year-over-year due to wet drawpoint problems in the underground block cave.

headline: Ur-Energy FIRES UP Second Wyoming ISR Mine — Shirley Basin Uranium Production Commences
snippet: Ur-Energy has begun capturing uranium-bearing solution at its Shirley Basin ISR project in Wyoming, its second active mine, expanding the company's combined licensed capacity to 4.2 million lbs U₃O₈ annually alongside its Lost Creek operation.

headline: Contango Silver & Gold Outlines $63M Alaska and Northern BC Exploration Push for 2026
snippet: Contango Silver & Gold plans $63 million in drilling and exploration across its consolidated portfolio of advanced gold and critical metals projects in Alaska and Northern British Columbia — one of the largest junior exploration programs in the region this year.

headline: BANK OF AMERICA TARGETS $135–$309 SILVER BY YEAR-END; Asian Metals Wrap Apr 24
snippet: GoldSeek's April 24 Asian metals update covers gold trading in the $4,700s while Bank of America projects silver reaching $135–$309/oz by end of 2026, citing tightening physical supply and accelerating industrial demand from solar and electronics sectors.

headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,738 — Up 41% Year-Over-Year as Dollar Slides and Hormuz Fears Linger
snippet: Gold held near $4,738/oz on April 23, up 41% year-over-year, as analysts at CLSA lifted their 2026 price target to $4,840/oz on sustained central bank accumulation and supply disruptions tied to the ongoing Hormuz closure.

headline: Discovery Mining PORCUPINE Posts 60,269 oz Gold in Q1 2026, On Track for 300,000 oz Full Year
snippet: Discovery Mining's Porcupine complex produced a quarterly record 60,269 oz of gold in Q1 2026, keeping the company firmly on track for its full-year production guidance of 260,000–300,000 oz.

headline: War, Hormuz Closure, China Acid Ban: The Triple Supply Shock Driving Gold, Silver, and Copper Higher
snippet: Gold, silver, and copper face overlapping supply squeezes from the Hormuz closure, China's pending sulfuric acid export ban, and six consecutive years of silver market deficits — three metals with bullish cases rooted in the same geopolitical catalyst.

headline: CANACCORD: Lithium Market Enters Deficit in 2026 — Won't Balance Until 2035
snippet: Canaccord analysts project a material lithium market deficit starting in 2026 and persisting through at least 2035, as two years of depressed prices have deferred the mine investment needed to match accelerating EV battery demand growth.

headline: Senate Votes Yes, But Minnesota Copper-Nickel Mine Still Years From Breaking Ground
snippet: Despite a Senate vote to open copper-nickel mining in Minnesota's Superior National Forest, analysts say remaining permitting, environmental review, and litigation hurdles mean commercial production is still many years away.

headline: FIRST NEW U.S. ISR URANIUM MINE IN 15 YEARS: Uranium Energy's Burke Hollow Begins Producing
snippet: Uranium Energy Corp's Burke Hollow ISR project in South Texas is now producing uranium — the first new in-situ recovery mine to come online in the U.S. in over a decade — with the Hobson processing plant licensed for up to 4 million lbs U₃O₈ per year.

headline: GOLD-SILVER RATIO RETREATS FROM 105 — History Says Silver Is About to Outrun Gold
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in April and is now contracting sharply — a historically reliable signal that silver is set to outperform gold — with spot silver facing key resistance at $78–$81 before the next leg higher.

headline: $2.8 BILLION: USA Rare Earth Buys Brazil's Serra Verde to Break China's Magnet Monopoly
snippet: USA Rare Earth agreed to acquire Serra Verde — the only operation outside Asia capable of producing all four magnetic rare earths at scale — for $300M cash and $127M in stock, with the combined entity targeting $1.8 billion in EBITDA by 2030.
